finding: E8 golden BOTH surfaces: ui+desktop types.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL, ui+desktop openapi.json canonically-equal (jq -S) vs .extraction/baseline; regen then restored via git checkout. Pure refactor confirmed — no wire surface moved.

ctx.auth()/user()/group()/session_settings() build a fresh repo from Arc<PgPool> per call; repos are stateless pool wrappers, so this is behaviourally identical to the cached global Repos accessor. ctx.pool() is the SAME Arc pool the factory was init'd with (layered from lib.rs/main.rs `pool`).

impl From<JwtConfig> for JwtSettings is a field-for-field move (secret/issuer/audience/access_hours/refresh_days/access_seconds). JwtService::try_new(impl Into) keeps every call site + `ziee::JwtConfig` test unchanged. jwt.rs unit tests (expiry override, debug-seconds seam, weak-secret) green under JwtSettings.

EventBusAuthSink.emit_user/emit_auth_provider wrap the raw module event into AppEvent::User/AppEvent::AuthProvider and call the real event_bus.emit_async — the SAME variant + fire-and-forget path as the former inline UserEvent::created(u)/AuthProviderEvent::x().into() sites, so every registered handler (e.g. assistant reading UserEvent::Created) still fires identically.

core::outbound re-exports the SAME url_validator fns; providers keep their cfg!(debug_assertions) DEV_LOCAL/PUBLIC_HTTP_OR_HTTPS policy branch verbatim + the redirect-disabled fallback client. No behavioural change to outbound HTTP.

storage_key is now a threaded param but is sourced app-side from crate::core::secrets::storage_key() at boot (build_auth_context) — same value. prepare_config_for_write's compat-mode branch (None key → plaintext, Some → encrypt+blank) is byte-identical. Secret is never widened or logged.

SSRF confinement preserved: apple/oauth2 outbound clients still route through the validated builder (via core::outbound) with the identical debug/release policy; validate_issuer_url/redirect/userinfo checks unchanged. No SSRF policy weakened.

OAuth2Provider now STORES the pool (was `_pool`, discarded) purely to build AuthRepository for oauth_session CRUD — the same rows the code touched before via global Repos.auth. No new query, no new data reached, sessions still provider-scoped.

AuthSyncSink.publish forwards the SAME (entity, action, id, audience, origin) tuple to the real sync::publish — audiences (perm::<GroupsRead>/UsersRead, owner(..)) and self-echo origin are unchanged, so no notification audience is widened (no cross-user leak).

Only added/renamed Extension extractors (non-body-consuming, order-independent) to handler signatures; no route path, method, DTO, permission, or response type changed — hence openapi.json + types.ts stay byte/canonical-identical (E8).

JwtService public surface preserved: try_new/new now take `impl Into<JwtSettings>`, and JwtConfig: Into<JwtSettings> via the app-side From, so ziee::JwtService::try_new(ziee::JwtConfig{..}) and desktop JwtService::new(config) still typecheck (verified: cargo check -p ziee + -p ziee-desktop exit 0).

refresh's claim_rotation_and_register + rotation_grace_successor now receive ctx.pool() — the identical Arc<PgPool>; the single-transaction rotation claim, row-lock, and 30s grace semantics are untouched.

Extension<AuthContext> is layered in EVERY router-build site that serves the auth/user handlers: lib.rs::setup_server (used by integration tests AND the desktop embed) and main.rs (the standalone bin). No handler can 500 on a missing extension. Integration test target compiles clean (cargo check --test integration_tests exit 0).

health.rs fns take pool + &dyn AuthEventSink; probe_provider builds create_provider from the passed pool (was Repos.pool().clone()); the AutoDisabled emit path + revert-to-disabled + record_test_result ordering are byte-identical.

Removed now-unused `use std::sync::Arc;` (user.rs) and the unused `pub use context::{...}` re-export (auth/mod.rs) surfaced by -D unused-imports. cargo check -p ziee + -p ziee-desktop: zero NEW warnings (only pre-existing KB/notifications/llm_repository dead-code warns remain).

SessionSettings DTO + the /api/auth/session-settings GET/PUT bodies unchanged; only the handler's data source moved from Repos to ctx.session_settings(); sync emit (SessionSettings/Update/nil/perm::<SessionSettingsRead>) preserved.
